steps.title: Match the modifier to its subject
steps.content: The opening phrase “Fortifying soil in arid ecosystems against erosion” describes what biocrusts do. The subject that follows the comma must therefore be “these crusts,” not a study, an estimate, or a reduction.

steps.title: Eliminate misplaced subjects
steps.content: Choice A makes “a recent study’s estimate” do the fortifying. Choice B makes “an estimated 60 percent reduction” do the fortifying. Choice D makes “a recent study” do the fortifying. The only choice that places “these crusts” after the modifier is grammatically logical. The answer is C.
